architecture.summary: aiTPR combines regional CNN features (from R-CNN object detection) with learned interaction tensors using Tensor Product Representation (TPR). The system extracts region-based visual features, derives orthogonal interaction embeddings from these features, and fuses them through an 'Assembled Selector Layer' before decoding captions via LSTM. Two variants are proposed: Early aiTPR fuses attributes and interactions early in the network, while Late aiTPR keeps them separate until fusion. The mechanism maintains orthogonality to prevent feature collapse into undefined spaces, and treats interactions as learnable tensor entities rather than simple weighted combinations.
trained_components: Assembled Selector Layer (attribute and interaction extraction)
learnable orthogonal transformation matrices for TPR

biblio.title: aiTPR: Attribute Interaction-Tensor Product Representation for Image Caption

claims.statement: Late aiTPR with Equation 33 achieves CIDEr-D of 1.401
Late aiTPR with Equation 33 achieves BLEU-4 of 0.484
Late aiTPR with Equation 33 achieves METEOR of 0.320
Late aiTPR with Equation 33 achieves SPICE of 0.233
Late aiTPR performed better than Early aiTPR and competing variants because the influence of regional feature combinations and their interactions helped generate better captions
aiTPR maintains orthogonality in feature space and prevents summation to undefined or null spaces that occur in prior methods using simple weighted addition
The paper introduces for the first time the notion of combining regional image features and abstracted interaction likelihood embedding for image captioning
aiTPR addresses the object-combination problem where CNN networks fail to differentiate similar object combinations by providing unique solutions through interaction tensors
